secondary_research_themes: Statistical Rigor in Machine Learning Evaluations
Large Language Models in SE (ChatGPT, Transformers)
methodological_traits: Building infrastructure for reproducible open science
Rigorous statistical benchmarking and hypothesis testing
communication_indicators: Focuses heavily on validity threats and replicability
researcher_summary: Steffen Herbold is a leading figure in empirical software engineering, distinguished by his focus on improving the foundational data and methodologies used by the broader research community. Rather than just applying machine learning to SE, his work frequently audits the quality of the datasets (exposing tangled commits and SZZ flaws) and the statistical methods used to evaluate them. He builds large-scale infrastructures (SmartSHARK, Autorank) to enforce replicability, open science, and statistical correctness. His research identity is that of a strict, highly meticulous empirical validator.
